senses.definition: the plural form of sclera, the tough white outer covering of the eyeball (except the cornea).
examples: In anatomy, the scleras protect the eye.
The doctor discussed the scleras during the eye exam.

senses.definition: producing or causing the formation of hard tissue.
examples: This condition is considered scleratogenous.
The treatment aims to control scleratogenous activity.

senses.definition: in zoology, a hard skeletal element (a spicule) found in sponges.
examples: The microscope showed several sclere within the sponge.
Sclere help form the sponge’s skeleton.

senses.definition: a disease or condition involving enlargement or abnormal widening of the sclera.
examples: The report mentioned sclerectasia as part of the diagnosis.
Sclerectasia may be linked to other eye problems.

senses.definition: the plural form of sclerectomy, a surgical procedure involving removal or cutting of part of the sclera.
examples: The patient has had previous sclerectomies.
Sclerectomies are sometimes used for specific eye conditions.

senses.definition: a surgical operation that removes or cuts part of the sclera.
examples: The ophthalmologist recommended a sclerectomy.
After the sclerectomy, the patient needed follow-up care.

senses.definition: swelling or thickening of tissues related to the sclera, especially described in medical use.
examples: Scleredema was listed among the findings.
The symptoms suggested scleredema.

senses.definition: a sclereid, a type of plant cell with thick, hard walls that gives strength and support.
examples: Sclereids make some fruits feel rough or hard.
The plant tissue contained many sclereids.

senses.definition: an alternative form (or older/variant form) of sclereid, a hard supporting plant cell.
examples: In the text, sclereide is used to refer to sclereid cells.
The author described sclereide in the fruit’s structure.

senses.definition: the plural form of sclereide, referring to multiple hard supporting plant cells.
examples: The sample showed many sclereides.
Sclereides contribute to the fruit’s hardness.

senses.definition: the plural form of sclereid, a plant cell that provides strength and support through thick, hard walls.
examples: Many sclereids are found in seed coats.
The nut’s texture comes from sclereids.

senses.definition: a medical condition characterized by hardening (thickening) of tissues, often used with context in dermatology or pediatrics.
examples: The baby was diagnosed with sclerema.
Sclerema involves hard, tight skin.

senses.definition: the plural form of sclerema, a condition involving hardening or thickening of tissues.
examples: Different scleremas may have different causes.
The study discussed several scleremas.

senses.definition: a medical term for a condition involving hardening of brain tissue.
examples: Sclerencephalia is described as brain tissue hardening in medical texts.
Doctors used the term to label the condition.

senses.definition: in botany, a plant tissue type made of cells with thick, lignified walls that provides strength and support.
examples: Sclerenchyma helps plants stay strong and upright.
The leaf contains sclerenchyma fibers.

senses.definition: the plural form of sclerenchyma, meaning multiple plant tissue areas that provide strength and support.
examples: The stem had sclerenchymas in several regions.
Different sclerenchymas appear in the plant’s structure.

senses.definition: a plural form of sclerenchyma used in some technical or older botanical writing.
examples: The book uses sclerenchymata as a term for strengthening tissues.
Sclerenchymata are described in the plant’s anatomy.

senses.definition: made of, belonging to, or relating to sclerenchyma (plant strength-support tissue with thick walls).
examples: The sclerenchymatous layer formed a hard boundary.
Sclerenchymatous tissue is important for support.

senses.definition: a variant or older spelling of sclerenchyma, referring to the plant strengthening tissue.
examples: The older text uses sclerenchyme for the same tissue concept as sclerenchyma.
Sclerenchyme provides support in the plant.

senses.definition: a substance (a pigment or chemical) named in biological/chemical contexts, associated with hard or colored biological materials.
examples: The analysis mentioned sclererythrin as one of the compounds.
Sclererythrin appears in specialized scientific descriptions.

senses.definition: the plural form of sclere, meaning multiple hard skeletal elements (spicules) in sponges.
examples: The scleres contribute to the sponge’s skeleton.
Under magnification, the scleres were clearly visible.

senses.definition: a technical term for a hard biological/biomineral substance named in specialized scientific contexts.
examples: The material’s components included scleretinite.
Scleretinite is mentioned in detailed analyses of hard tissues.

senses.definition: the plural or related form used in some scientific writings for sclera, the tough white outer covering of the eyeball (except the cornea).
examples: The atlas shows the scleria surrounding the eyeball.
Doctors may refer to the scleria when describing eye anatomy.

senses.definition: plural form of scleriasis, used as a technical term referring to hardening conditions (medical usage).
examples: The paper lists several scleriases.
Scleriases are discussed in clinical descriptions.

senses.definition: a medical condition involving hardening or thickening of tissue.
examples: Scleriasis can make affected tissue feel firm.
The diagnosis used the term scleriasis.
